item.title: Surprise! I became a Minister last year so I could volunteer to legally marry couples with Star Wars-themed weddings. https://youtu.be/oP3h5J90goo
item.description: In exchange for having (F)luke Skywalker marry them,(sort of like they did on The Big Bang Theory) the couple made a nice donation to several charities. This video is from the first one. Make sure to see the middle at around 14:30 in and also the end as well. https://youtu.be/oP3h5J90goo EDIT - Most Viral? Thank you, my Dudes. Out of all the Social Media I'm on, the people here are THE BEST, kindest and most generous of all. Imgurians are awesome!
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker

item.title: Here are 20 nice things to make you feel nice. Nice.
item.description:
Welcome to day 4 of Puckett Week! Let's get nice. Here are 20 nice things to make you feel nice. Nice. https://imgur.com/gallery/qTI40vC The 13th set of posts - Here are 20 nice things to make you feel nice. Nice https://imgur.com/a/vdbnjWN The 12th set of posts - Here are 20 nice things to make you feel nice. Nice https://imgur.com/a/nnwcdwr The 11th set of posts - Here are 20 nice things to make you feel nice. Nice https://imgur.com/a/ZpKGByS The 10th set of posts - Here are 20 nice things to make you feel nice. Nice https://imgur.com/a/redduMH The 9th set of posts - Here are 20 nice things to make you feel nice. Nice. https://imgur.com/a/IZbBOqF
@PorkChop2285 creating works of art from Imgurian pictures I love it!
Did my puppy write this?
@KingofthePlatypi created this amazing image of my family for TheFreePizzaDude. It's so good to be back with that project, some of the nicest people I've never met.
Appreciate everyone being so nice this week in the comment section
Tomorrow is the anniversary of Mr Puckett the dog passing away. In his honor we've been doing a fundraiser and Supply Drive for domestic violence survivors facing homelessness. Domestic violence is the leading cause of homelessness for women and children in America, and this week we can help.
My 6 year old, 16 year old, and 2 year old puppies The Pucketts ... The Mary Parrish Center provides free housing for survivors to leave their abusers and stay off the street. In Mr Puckett's honor, I'm asking anyone who can afford it to donate $15 to Mary Parrish. https://www.maryparrish.org/donate I also had them set up an Amazon Wishlist if you'd like to send items directly to survivors https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/3USHUHIVSINQE?ref_=wl_share ... Thank you all so much, I'll see everyone tomorrow.
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: Imagine what she could get if she started making coffee at home!

item.title: What kind of watermelon is this?
item.dc:creator: WeirdAlisMySpiritUserName

item.title: Groom surprises his bride on the altar with unexpected guests
item.description:
Mechanical engineer José Victor Flach moved his fiancée Cíntia Bonfante Pereira to tears during their wedding ceremony in Caxias do Sul Brazil, with a touching surprise. The video captured the moment Cíntia, a speech therapist, turned around to see her students with Down's syndrome meet her down the aisle. The couple then took a moment to say hello to each of the children, making them feel special on the occasion, as the emotional crowd looked on. All the girls wore white dresses with colourful bows and flowers in their hair. One held on to a tiny bouquet of flowers while another could be seen with a large lollipop. Two cheeky boys made quite the entrance when they followed the group in an electric car, while minders made sure they 'drove' in the right direction. Cíntia, who could not have been more touched, fought back tears of joy as she got to the children's level to greet them.
She revealed she had always wanted to have the children involved in her wedding, but her groom had planned the whole thing to be a surprise. 'It was very emotional because I asked the priest from the church we got married to let me have the kids I had in my practice, but he said that I couldn’t. 'In reality my husband had asked him to say that because he had already planned it with the priest. 'I truly love what I do and I am lucky to be able to help them have a better quality of life. I believe that every person has potential,' she added.
'We need to look at them beyond their disabilities so then we can help them surpass the barriers they have in life.' 'I love my profession so much and having the opportunity to have some of the children with me on this day was inexplicable, the emotion overcame me in a way that I thought my heart would not take! 'Immensely happy with the repercussion of this video because my dream has always been to spread the beauty of social and school inclusion, and this video, without meaning to, became the living proof that everyone deserves equal opportunities! 'And that is what I have always fought for! And with this visibility that the video is having we are able to show how much more beautiful everything is when we include everyone,' she said.
1. Kudos to the husband for making a special day MORE special for his bride. 2. I needed to clear my tear ducts and this helped. 3. God Bless Cintia and her commitment to helping those with special needs. 4 just found out somelese made a post about this not too long ago. https://imgur.com/gallery/33owzrq Usually when I find this I delete my post, but I will keep it up, yo can chosoe to upvote/downvote/nothing. Sorry I did not do my due diligence.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Day 265: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Mac Burger 8oz Angus Beef Patty, Melted Cheddar Cheese Sitting On Top Of A Fried Mac-N-Cheese Patty With House Mac-n-cheese, Ainsworth Special Sauce, Toasted Brioche Bun From The Ainsworth In New York (And A Few Other Locations Rockville Centre, Phoenix, Chelsea, East Village, Hoboken, Nashville) Thank You @ramzus2 For This Burger Submission! 12/10 Previous Day 264: https://imgur.com/gallery/onZKKNi MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! item.title: So I did a thing (Day 2)
item.description:
Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/VjFNr0y Ignoring workers' rights opened a can of worms. While I am waiting for the NLRB agent to get back to me about my pending filed charge, it was made sure that every coworker knows about their rights to discuss wages. Office manager was out for the day, so getting this 'policy change' officially posted (and subsiquentially recorded for recordkeeping) will take another day. Thus, I am making this post to put together my findings. Before the incident, I have gathered that what had happened were three different situations. The most important of them being where a patron asked an employee how much they made. They answered honestly which caused an uproar with said patron, who was a prominent VIP of our workplace (we are a semi-membership-based country club.) Details afterward were uncertain, but seemed to be the final straw tat made this policy become into place. The second incident was when our secondary manager has left, leaving a wide gap in responsibility and pay, which the former was dumped onto another coworker, and the latter was conveniently withheld. Tensions rose with this decision, especially with the amount of work being added with little to no increase in pay. Long arguments about this situation consequentially became commonplace with the fact that no one became fired means that the boss actually *needs* us. The last incident is when a newly-hired coworker who went above and beyond their job description is making at least 50% more in wages than their coworkers, but that wasn't exactly troubling, just how the information got brought up and began spreading.
Now that I knew that the bosses didn't want us discussing our wages, I began to directly ask others. Some haven't had a raise in 13 years of being here. I am still making the minimum amount for my position after 3 and brand new hires make the same amount as I do. This discussion allows us to better gauge our worth which will better allow us to negotiate our compensation. This is a small business with barely 20 people if that many here at once. The turnover is so high that it is difficult to determine the actual number currently employed or in the system. Though it is also a multimillion dollar business which has greatly improved due to the efforts of the team who does stay.
Every accusation is an admission of guilt as they say. It turns out that our boss has been repeatedly fired or forced to quit from other smaller businesses in our industry due to moments where invoices are ran a bit too high, there is missing money in a safe, or flat out giving away supplies for free to their friends. In a personal incident, there was a patron who enjoyed the product we have provided for them and gave our boss $1000 to split amongst the team. I got $20, as did four of my other coworkers. Other incidents include a check that was given by the patron who specified that it was meant to be included into the commission pool for all fo our coworkers (all non-manager front-of-house staff get a shared 22% commission on events.) We didn't see a cent of that even a month later. In either case, I now wait beside my phone, hoping that the National Labor Relations Board agent would call me back soon, because there is so much going on that I am not sue if I should call them again, or the news. I apologize for the incomplete recollection of the events and situation. This is as much as I can remember right now and putting it into words would allow for me to better recall the details. But yeah, it seems like our boss tried to intimidate us by forgetting that we had rights, only for that to slowly blow up in his face. We will see what happens. Edit: I heard back from my board agent! The charges have been docketed and I have a case number and everything! On top of that, I have another agent talking to me about how to join the Service Employees International Union and have just sent my paperwork! Things are moving fast!
item.dc:creator: 1garret322

item.title: You remember all the money th govt gave companies and not the people?
item.description: Left the watermark for those who are curious about changing how work is Sauce: https://www.reddit.com/r/antiwork/comments/supswe/remember_when_the_government_loaned_billions_of/?utm_medium=android_app&utm_source=share Between the threat of wage slavery and starvation I don't really see a "choice"
item.dc:creator: TheChubbyDragon

item.title: Remember when he downplayed the link between smoking and cancer and then died of cancer?
item.description: Not just any cancer, lung cancer. After claiming that lung cancer "takes 50 years to kill people, if it does", it killed him in a single year from the first 'shortness of breath' indication that led to his diagnosis. Almost as if hubris is an accelerant...
item.dc:creator: ButtBot9900
